mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or flat, discount-rate pressure eases and equity valuations expand, so S&P 500 returns over the next month are positive. The signal is falsified if lower policy rates are followed by systematically negative 20-day returns.
failure_condition: If triggered months with declining FEDFUNDS do not show a higher median 20-day SPX return than non-triggered months, or if the hit rate is below 55% over any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates weakening household income and demand, which should depress S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when unemployment rises but equities still rally because markets are already pricing recession risk.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-spike episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average return after the trigger is not statistically below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS signals broadening labor income and retail demand, which supports earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is not active when only one of the two series improves.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-positive PAYEMS and RSAFS state does not outperform each single-series state in median 20-day SPX returns, or if the combined state has Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO accelerates while CPIAUCSL stays contained, real activity is improving without an inflation shock, so earnings expectations rise and S&P 500 returns are positive over the next quarter. The signal fails if output growth is accompanied by renewed inflation pressure.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation stays below its 6-month average while INDPRO rises
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO impulses with stable CPIAUCSL do not produce positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average return is not greater than the unconditional 60-day mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in HOUST marks housing-cycle acceleration and higher cyclically sensitive demand, which should lift equities over the next month. The effect should disappear when housing starts are rising only because of volatile seasonal noise.
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start surges are followed by negative or zero median 20-day SPX returns, or if the trigger has no incremental predictive value after controlling for the recent market trend.

mechanism_narrative: In a restrictive-rate environment, elevated FEDFUNDS suppresses risk appetite and compresses equity multiples, so the S&P 500 should underperform over the next 5 trading days. The effect is strongest when rates are clearly above neutral.
failure_condition: Reject if high-FEDFUNDS episodes do not produce negative 5-day SPX returns on average, or if the signal loses sign consistency in a tightening-cycle sample.

mechanism_narrative: A combined slowdown in PAYEMS growth and rising UNRATE identifies labor-market deterioration before earnings revisions turn negative, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next quarter. The signal is invalid if one labor metric worsens without the other.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS weakness and UNRATE increases do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power versus single proxies.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive after a contractionary period, the economy is entering a recovery phase that improves earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect fails if GDP rebounds while consumer demand and labor data remain weak.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 turns positive after at least one negative quarter
failure_condition: Reject if GDP rebound episodes do not show higher 20-day SPX returns than the unconditional sample, or if the effect disappears when RSAFS and PAYEMS fail to confirm the recovery.
